commands.review_summary:
The PR allows validated generated `.html` host-read attachments under the resolved OpenClaw temp root, adds media regression tests, and adds `@openclaw/net-policy` workspace aliases for source plugin loads.

PR surface: Source +114, Tests +122. Total +236 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, but not from an executed live repro: current main rejects host-read `.html` with `path-not-allowed`, and Telegram outbound media uses that shared loader path. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This active PR has a protected maintainer label, a security-boundary decision, missing live Telegram proof, and a dirty branch that maintainers cannot directly modify, so it needs maintainer/contributor handling rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(media): allow trusted generated html attachments
target.body:
## Summary

- Allows host-read `.html` attachments only when they are trusted generated reports under the resolved OpenClaw temp root.
- Keeps arbitrary host-local HTML blocked, including symlink and hardlink attempts into the temp root.
- Adds regression coverage for trusted generated HTML, outside-root HTML, non-HTML text, symlink, hardlink, and binary disguised `.html` cases.
- Adds `@openclaw/net-policy` to source plugin loader aliases so jiti-loaded plugin SDK subpaths resolve the new workspace package in agent CI shards.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media/web-media.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media/load-options.test.ts src/media/read-capability.test.ts src/media/outbound-attachment.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-runner/execute.supervisor-capture.test.ts src/agents/sessions/extensions/loader.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/sdk-alias.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media/web-media.test.ts src/media/load-options.test.ts src/media/read-capability.test.ts src/media/outbound-attachment.test.ts src/plugins/sdk-alias.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `env -u OPENCLAW_TESTBOX pnpm check:changed`
- `/Users/steipete/Projects/agent-scripts/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram delivery of generated HTML report attachments regressed after PR #86531 because host-read `.html` files were blocked and fell back to Markdown. This change restores generated HTML report attachment delivery without reopening arbitrary host-local HTML reads.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/telegram-generated-html-attachments`; remote Testbox check also passed before the autoreview hardening follow-up, and the final hardened diff passed local changed gates.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media/web-media.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media/load-options.test.ts src/media/read-capability.test.ts src/media/outbound-attachment.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-runner/execute.supervisor-capture.test.ts src/agents/sessions/extensions/loader.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/sdk-alias.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media/web-media.test.ts src/media/load-options.test.ts src/media/read-capability.test.ts src/media/outbound-attachment.test.ts src/plugins/sdk-alias.test.ts --reporter=verbose
env -u OPENCLAW_TESTBOX pnpm check:changed
/Users/steipete/Projects/agent-scripts/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  64 passed (64)
```

```text
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  17 passed (17)
```

```text
Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests  16 passed (16)
```

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  71 passed (71)
```

```text
Test Files  5 passed (5)
Tests  152 passed (152)
```

```text
Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
```

```text
autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
overall: patch is correct (0.86)
```

Observed result after fix: valid generated `.html` reports under the OpenClaw temp root load as `text/html` documents for host-read delivery; `.html` files outside that root, symlinked files, hardlinked files, non-HTML text, and binary disguised as HTML are rejected with `path-not-allowed`. Source plugin jiti loads also resolve `@openclaw/net-policy/*` workspace subpaths through the shared alias map.

What was not tested: live Telegram Bot API delivery, because channel credentials were not available in this checkout.
target.author: steipete
